title: Synostemon bacciformis
intro: Synostemon bacciformis is a plant species native to Western Australia, also known as 'Finger Lime Bush', belonging to the family Acanthaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various conditions such as wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Synostemon bacciformis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Synostemon bacciformis is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture to harness its health benefits.
taxonomy: Synostemon bacciformis is classified within the plant family Phyllanthaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the Anacampserotoideae subfamily. The Phyllanthaceae family is part of the order Malpighiales, which is a large and diverse group of plants. Synostemon bacciformis is native to Western Australia and is a shrub or small tree. The classification of Synostemon bacciformis is also known as the Anacampserotoideae subfamily within the Phyllanthaceae family.
